items.title: 'Caught lying': Police bashed Brad, then charged him with assaulting them

children.content: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.

gallery.altText: A man winces in agony in the corner of a police holding cell as three officers stand over him.

thumbnail.altText: A man winces in agony in the corner of a police holding cell as three officers stand over him.

items.title: Two questions still hang above the now-decaying federal budget

image.altText: Jim Chalmers and Anthony Albanese stand next to each other; both wear dark suit jackets.

items.title: Young Australians reject budget as One Nation popularity climbs further

children.content: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they would rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.

image.altText: A woman with short red hair and a blue blazer with a brooch

items.title: Australia the 'perfect' target for new Islamist terror network

children.content: Australia would be the "perfect" target for a fledgling Islamist terror network that has carried out antisemitic attacks in Europe, the UK and Canada, experts have warned.

items.title: Liberals grapple with age, gender problems in new party paper

children.content: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.

items.title: Australian housing market stalls in May

children.content: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.

items.title: Australia scraps new Virginia-class submarines in AUKUS shake-up

children.content: The US, UK and Australia have announced they will "streamline" the purchase, with Australia no longer buying new submarines, and acquiring three second-hand ones.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize castle

children.content: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

image.altText: Three soldiers stand looking at an old crumbling structure in the near distance

items.title: Ian Thorpe's mythic rise from boy to hero had an ironic twist

children.content: Ian Thorpe become a global star as a teenager, attracting both opportunities and scrutiny. Now, he wishes he'd done some things differently.

items.title: Virgin customers have one month to use COVID travel credits

items.title: Brazil authorities investigate two possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows

children.content: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

items.title: Dozens killed by blast in rebel-held Myanmar village

children.content: Authorities say the blast was likely caused by explosives used for mining that had been stored in a warehouse.

image.altText: The remnants of a house standing with debris scattered around it, in the distance a destroyed neighbourhood.

items.title: Panthers hang on as Alamoti makes amends in remarkable Warriors clash

children.content: Penrith wing Paul Alamoti makes up for a horror defensive error with a spectacular winning try as the Panthers down the Warriors 20-18 in an exhilarating top-of-the-table clash.

items.title: Divers at Laos cave search for two men thought to be trapped further down

children.content: The remaining four men from a group of five were freed from the flooded cave on Saturday evening, in extraordinary and emotional scenes, as search efforts continue for two people thought to still be trapped inside.

items.title: Barnaby Joyce's link to how e-bikes came to be everywhere

children.content: When Barnaby Joyce was deputy prime minister and transport minister in 2021, his assistant minister Kevin Hogan made a quiet, but hugely significant change to the Road Vehicle Standards Act.

items.title: Horses bolt through Rome after police officer sets off fireworks

children.content: Fireworks set off without permission by a police officer during a rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting through the capital and injuring several riders.

image.altText: A white horse with full riding gear but no rider runs down a paved street at night.

items.title: Lucky escape for pair as tree falls on car amid WA wild weather

children.content: Two people have had a lucky escape after a tree fell onto their car on a major highway in WA's South West this afternoon, as strong winds lashed the region.

gallery.altText: Emergency vehicle and personel at the scene

thumbnail.altText: Emergency vehicle and personel at the scene

items.title: Budget won't be 'main driver' if house prices fall, housing minister says

children.content: The housing minister says the federal budget will have only a "mild" impact on house prices, and if predictions bear out that house prices may drop by 10 per cent, that won't be due to the government's tax changes.

image.altText: a female politician wearing a white blazer talking

items.title: Iran's internet revival unearths videos regime 'didn't want world to witness'

children.content: After being cut off from the world for more than 12 weeks, Iranians have come back online. Their initial reactions are joy, shock and confusion.

image.altText: An image of burning infrastructure in Iran as people stand infront of it and watch on.

items.title: Hundreds arrested as Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League win sparks riots

children.content: More than 50 police officers are wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalise shops, with one small group even trying to storm a Paris police station.

image.altText: Police are silhouetted as a firework explodes on a Paris street.

items.title: Applications to protect Victoria Park rejected ahead of construction of Olympic stadium

children.content: The federal government has rejected two applications for protection of Brisbane's Victoria Park ahead of construction on the Olympic stadium beginning tomorrow, but is still considering another five.

items.title: Why are Australians still getting lost and dying in the outback?

children.content: Kelly's son and ex-husband died "stupid" deaths in the Australian outback. She's on a mission to make sure no more lives are lost.
